description: 8 mesocycles x 6 weeks each (intro/deload + build + peak), 4 lifting days per week, fixed day structure within each mesocycle. Monday and Thursday lower body, Tuesday and Friday upper body. Mesocycle 1 upper body is arms-only due to post-surgery restrictions.

progression_model.week_0: Intro/deload week: 1 set per exercise, stop well short of failure.
progression_model.week_1: Establish baseline loads, keep prescribed RIR.
progression_model.week_2: Add reps within target range before adding load.
progression_model.week_3: Increase load if all sets hit top of rep range at target RIR.
progression_model.week_4: Push toward lower end of prescribed RIR range.
progression_model.week_5: Peak week: maintain exercise selection, use planned intensity techniques where programmed.

mesocycles.name: Recovery / Reintroduction
mesocycles.goal: Post-surgery reintroduction, tissue tolerance, technique practice, low fatigue.

notes: No failure work.
Use slow eccentrics and controlled pauses to rebuild movement quality.
Upper body is arms only.

exercises.details: 3-second lower, 2-second squeeze at top, controlled up. Prioritize glute lockout without lumbar extension.
Slow stretch-focused eccentric, brief pause near stretch, controlled concentric.
Controlled curl with 2-second squeeze in shortened position.
Constant tension, controlled outward drive, no bouncing.
Controlled descent, brief bottom pause, drive through front heel.
Wide stance with controlled lowering and no lockout bounce.
Strict curl, controlled eccentric, keep elbows stable.
Emphasize the lengthened position and full elbow extension under control.
Keep elbows pinned, slight spread at lockout.
Long eccentric, brief pause, controlled drive.
Lower with control, moderate stance, full foot pressure.
Controlled descent, slight forward torso if needed for comfort, drive through midfoot.
Controlled raise with 2-second squeeze at top.
Controlled stride length and smooth transitions.
Same movement pattern as Tuesday to keep recovery predictable.
Strict tempo, emphasize elbow flexor control.

mesocycles.name: Hypertrophy Base
mesocycles.goal: Rebuild full training volume, establish baseline hypertrophy loading.

notes: Compounds use controlled eccentrics.
Isolation work uses smooth continuous tension.
Increase reps before load when possible.

exercises.details: Controlled down, brief peak squeeze, more explosive concentric.
Maintain stretch tension and neutral spine.
Stable front leg loading, controlled descent.
Controlled lower, light pause, press smoothly.
Drive elbow down, control full stretch.
Slight pause at top, slow lowering.
Lower under control, avoid elbow flare.
Control descent, smooth drive through front leg.
Controlled eccentric and no rebound out of bottom.
Longer lowering to improve stability and quad loading.
Controlled eccentric, press with stable bar path.
Controlled depth and strong lockout within comfort.

mesocycles.name: Hypertrophy + Drop Sets
mesocycles.goal: Increase metabolic stress while holding volume in check.

notes: Drop sets are reserved for lower-risk isolation exercises.
Use only on the last set of the programmed exercises.
Reduce load about 25-30% and continue to near failure.

exercises.details: Heavy but controlled. No intensity method.
Controlled squeeze. No drop set here this meso.
Last set drop set: reduce 25% and continue with constant tension.
Last set drop set: reduce 25%, then finish with controlled partials in top half.
Strict work, no drop set this meso.
Last set drop set: reduce 30% and continue to near failure with full range.
Last set drop set: reduce 25% and continue with strict reps only.
Controlled top position. No drop set this meso.

mesocycles.name: Strength + Eccentric Control
mesocycles.goal: Increase mechanical tension through heavier loading and longer eccentrics on compounds.

notes: Heavier compounds use longer eccentrics.
Isolation remains controlled but not exaggerated.
Progress load before extra reps when form stays clean.

exercises.details: Heavy loading with slow eccentric and solid top lockout.
Very controlled lowering to emphasize stretch-mediated tension.
Controlled stretch and deliberate elbow drive.
Strict control in the lengthened range.
Slow lower, pause, drive out smoothly.
Long eccentric and stable pressing path.
Controlled range and stable rib position.

mesocycles.name: Hypertrophy + Myo Reps
mesocycles.goal: Maximize hypertrophy stimulus efficiently with low-to-moderate volume.

global_rules.rir_range: 1-2 on standard sets; activation set near failure on myo work
notes: Use myo reps on low-skill isolation movements only.
Activation set should stop around 0-1 reps in reserve.
Mini-sets use 10-15 seconds rest.

exercises.details: First set is activation set, then 3 mini-sets of 3-5 reps with 10-15 sec rest.
Use lighter load, go near failure on activation set, then short-rest clusters.
Activation set near failure, then three mini-sets with short rest.
Activation set near failure, then short-rest mini-sets with strict form.

mesocycles.name: Strength + Drop Set Hybrid
mesocycles.goal: Emphasize heavier compounds while using strategic finishers for extra hypertrophy stimulus.

notes: Main lifts are heavy and controlled.
Only isolation work gets drop sets.
Keep technique strict even in fatigue.

exercises.details: Last set drop set: reduce 25-30% and continue with full ROM.
Constant tension, no drop set this meso.
Last set drop set: reduce 25% and continue with strict reps.
Last set drop set: reduce load 20-25% and continue with controlled reps.
Last set drop set: reduce 30% and continue to near failure.
Last set drop set: reduce 25% and continue with strict form.

mesocycles.name: Advanced Hypertrophy
mesocycles.goal: Peak hypertrophy phase using one myo-rep movement and one drop-set movement per day.

notes: Do not stack myo reps and drop sets on the same exercise.
Keep compounds controlled and repeatable.
High fatigue management phase, so execution matters.

exercises.details: Activation set near failure, then short-rest clusters.
Last set drop set: reduce 25% and continue with constant tension.
Activation set near failure, then short-rest clusters.
Last set drop set: reduce 25% and continue with strict reps.
Activation set near failure, then short-rest mini-sets.
Last set drop set: reduce bodyweight or dumbbell load only if safe and practical; otherwise perform standard sets.
Activation set near failure, then short-rest clusters with strict form.
Last set drop set: reduce 20-25% and continue with controlled reps.

mesocycles.name: Consolidation / Recomp
mesocycles.goal: Maintain muscle, manage fatigue, and consolidate progress.

notes: Back off fatigue slightly.
Keep execution clean and consistent.
Use this phase to recover while maintaining output.
